guide_review_response.work_item: 2.4.v2 (verification surfaced finding f1 in Stage-2.1 chokepoint helper)

reviewing.finding_title: Chokepoint guard `_require_project_id` accepts 0 and negative integers (§D.1 leak)

guide_review_response.summary:
EA-4 correctly applied the scope-expansion-stop-and-report rule (g4-2 / w14) and surfaced a real §D.1 leak in `tools/_vikunja_client.py:28-32`: the chokepoint helper `_require_project_id` rejects None / bool / str / float / non-int but PASSES 0 and negative ints. Confirmed on disk; the rejection condition is exactly what EA-4 reported. Live Vikunja smoke (per EA-4) confirmed `GET /projects/0/tasks` and `GET /projects/-1/tasks` return 200 OK with data -- the §D.1 "all tasks" gate is bypassed for any non-positive int caller.
Disposition: APPROVE Option (a) -- fix now in 2.4 closure with explicit Lead Architect (LA) authorization. Two reasons override EA-4's Option (b) recommendation:
(1) PATH CORRECTION: EA-4's reasoning that "2.6 already touches _vikunja_client.py per g5-4" conflates two different files. g5-4 disposes of `tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py` (the LEGACY pure-HTTP client). f1 is in `tools/_vikunja_client.py` (the NEW chokepoint, Stage 2.1 deliverable). The 2.6 work plan does NOT inherently touch the chokepoint guard. Deferring f1 to 2.6 means EA-5 (or whoever picks up 2.6) must remember to fix the chokepoint helper IN ADDITION TO closing the legacy file -- which is a high drop-the-ball risk.
(2) RULE APPLICABILITY: The scope-expansion-stop-and-report rule (g4-2 / w14) was authored to prevent additive scope (e.g., adding critical_notify.ps1 to the canonical 7 PS1, or expanding the chokepoint audit beyond v3-handoff scope). f1 is NOT additive scope -- it is a DEFECT IN ALREADY-IN-SCOPE work (the Stage 2.1 chokepoint helper that file 13 chose to route through). Defect closure of a §D.1 HARD RULE violation is on a different scope axis than additive scope expansion. EA-4 was right to STOP and REPORT (the rule mandates that); LA is right to AUTHORIZE the fix now (option (a)) rather than defer.
Single Stage-2 commit at 2.9 absorbs ALL Stage-2 work regardless of when chronologically it was authored. So "fixing in 2.4 closure" vs "fixing in 2.6" doesn't affect commit isolation -- both end up in the same fold-everything commit. EA-4's concern about "breaking the file-13-only review framing of the upcoming single commit" is misframed: the commit at 2.9 has no file-13-only framing; it's a broad multi-item commit per the v3 handoff commit_template. My Guide-#5 verification of file 13 already covered `tools/_vikunja_client.py` (I read it during w15 verification at the EA-3 mid-stage review). A 2-line fix is a small re-review increment, not a re-review of all 2.4 work.
Fix specifics: 2-line change to `_require_project_id` rejection condition + error message update + 2 new tests (test_require_project_id_rejects_zero, test_require_project_id_rejects_negative). Pytest baseline expected to move from 280 to 282 passed (NET-ADD per Stage 2 V matrix). Existing tests use only positive ints (3, 7) and floats; no breakage. Then continue with Item 2.5 + 2.5.v2 as previously authorized; re-evaluate budget at 2.5.v2 → 2.6 boundary.

independent_disk_verification_of_f1.check: git log --oneline -1: 9d17e7f -- ZERO new commits since prior Guide-#5 review. git status --porcelain: 24 entries (15 M + 2 D + 7 ??) -- unchanged count. Single-commit discipline (w13) holds.

Read tools/_vikunja_client.py lines 20-38:
def _require_project_id(project_id: Any) -> int: """Validate that ``project_id`` is an explicit ``int`` (not ``bool``).""" if ( project_id is None or isinstance(project_id, bool) or not isinstance(project_id, int) ): raise ProjectScopeError(...) return project_id
Rejection condition has 3 clauses: None / bool / not-int. NO clause for `project_id <= 0`. Therefore: `_require_project_id(0)` returns 0 (passes); `_require_project_id(-1)` returns -1 (passes); `_require_project_id(-99999)` returns -99999 (passes). EA-4's f1 finding is EMPIRICALLY CORRECT.
Provenance: This file was created at Stage 2.1 (commit history: introduced as part of EA-2's items 2.1+2.2 work, predating EA-3's 2.4 partial and EA-4's 2.4 closure). The cleanup-C3 docstring correctly notes the bool-subclass-of-int gotcha but doesn't address the <= 0 case. Pre-existing latent defect surfaced now by EA-4's 2.4.v2 live smoke test.

Read tools/tests/test_vikunja_client_scope.py (6 tests): - test_list_without_project_id_raises: project_id=None (still rejected with fix) - test_list_with_string_project_id_raises: project_id="1" (still rejected with fix) - test_list_with_bool_project_id_raises: project_id=True/False (still rejected with fix) - test_list_with_int_project_id_ok: project_id=3 (still PASSES with fix; 3 > 0) - test_require_project_id_returns_value_on_int: _require_project_id(7) (still PASSES; 7 > 0) - test_require_project_id_rejects_float: _require_project_id(3.0) (still rejected as non-int) ZERO existing tests break. Adding two new tests (rejects_zero, rejects_negative) takes the tools/ baseline from 280 → 282. Per v3 handoff <item id="2.7">: "Stage 2 should NET-ADD tests" -- this is consistent with the V matrix V2 expansion.

disposition_decision.option_evaluated: Fold the chokepoint guard strengthening into 2.4 closure NOW with explicit LA approval. Specific edits: 1. tools/_vikunja_client.py lines 28-37: extend rejection condition + update error message (verbatim suggested change in <suggested_fix> below). 2. tools/tests/test_vikunja_client_scope.py: add two tests (test_require_project_id_rejects_zero, test_require_project_id_rejects_negative). 3. Re-run pytest tools/ -- expect 282 passed (was 280; +2 new tests). 4. UNCOMMITTED on disk per single-commit-at-2.9 protocol; folds into the 2.9 commit. 5. EA-4 documents f1 disposition + the LA authorization in the eventual 2.10 STATUS.md append-only entry. Trade-offs: Adds 1 file to the working tree (tools/_vikunja_client.py becomes M alongside the 4 existing EA-4 M files). Adds 1 file to the test mod (tools/tests/test_vikunja_client_scope.py becomes M). Adds ~2 minutes of EA-4 work + Guide micro-re-review.

Defer f1 to 2.6. EA-4's framing was that "2.6 already touches _vikunja_client.py per g5-4." This is INCORRECT path-conflation: g5-4 is about `tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py` (the LEGACY pure-HTTP client); f1 is in `tools/_vikunja_client.py` (the NEW chokepoint, Stage 2.1 deliverable). Different files, different scope axes. The 2.6 work plan as currently scoped (extended grep + chokepoint routing across multiple files + g5-4 legacy fold-or-shim disposition + D3 dashboard_maintainer.py:113 closure + observation o1 handler-level-guard-vs-VikunjaClient-routing decision) does NOT inherently touch the chokepoint helper guard. Deferring f1 to 2.6 means EA-5 (or whoever picks up 2.6) must remember an additional task that isn't naturally surfaced by their reading of v3 handoff + iteration history. If EA-5 forgets, the §D.1 leak persists into the Stage 2.9 single commit -- Stage 2 closes with a known §D.1 violation. High drop-ball risk.
Surface to Guide-#5 first. EA-4 effectively did this by stopping-and-reporting + LA relaying to me. This response IS the Guide-#5 disposition.

suggested_fix.current_code:
def _require_project_id(project_id: Any) -> int: """Validate that ``project_id`` is an explicit ``int`` (not ``bool``).
``bool`` is a subclass of ``int`` in Python, so we must explicitly reject it BEFORE the ``isinstance(int)`` check to prevent ``project_id=True`` (which would resolve to ``project_id=1``) from bypassing the gate. This is the Stage 2 cleanup-C3 guard. """ if ( project_id is None or isinstance(project_id, bool) or not isinstance(project_id, int) ): raise ProjectScopeError( "SECURITY: Vikunja queries require an explicit int project_id " "(not bool, not str, not None). 'All tasks' queries are forbidden " f"by Master Plan §D.1. Got: {project_id!r}" ) return project_id
suggested_fix.new_code:
def _require_project_id(project_id: Any) -> int: """Validate that ``project_id`` is an explicit positive ``int`` (not ``bool``, not <= 0).
``bool`` is a subclass of ``int`` in Python, so we must explicitly reject it BEFORE the ``isinstance(int)`` check to prevent ``project_id=True`` (which would resolve to ``project_id=1``) from bypassing the gate. This is the Stage 2 cleanup-C3 guard.
Stage 2.4.v2 finding f1: Vikunja silently accepts non-positive project IDs (``GET /projects/0/tasks`` and ``GET /projects/-1/tasks`` both return 200 OK with data), so any non-positive int caller bypasses the §D.1 scope guard. Closed here by extending the rejection condition to ``project_id <= 0``. """ if ( project_id is None or isinstance(project_id, bool) or not isinstance(project_id, int) or project_id <= 0 ): raise ProjectScopeError( "SECURITY: Vikunja queries require an explicit positive int project_id " "(not bool, not str, not None, not <= 0). 'All tasks' queries are " f"forbidden by Master Plan §D.1. Got: {project_id!r}" ) return project_id

test_additions.append_at_end:
def test_require_project_id_rejects_zero(): """Stage 2.4.v2 finding f1: project_id=0 is non-positive and bypasses Vikunja's server-side scope (returns 200 OK with data). The chokepoint must reject.""" from tools._vikunja_client import _require_project_id
with pytest.raises(ProjectScopeError): _require_project_id(0)
def test_require_project_id_rejects_negative(): """Stage 2.4.v2 finding f1: any negative int is non-positive and bypasses Vikunja's server-side scope. The chokepoint must reject.""" from tools._vikunja_client import _require_project_id
with pytest.raises(ProjectScopeError): _require_project_id(-1) with pytest.raises(ProjectScopeError): _require_project_id(-99999)

suggested_fix.expected_pytest_result: .venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest tools/ --tb=short -q Expected: 282 passed (was 280; +2 new f1 tests). Zero existing-test breakage (verified via test inspection: all existing tests use positive ints 3 / 7, or floats / strings / bools / None which remain correctly rejected).

ack.topic: f1 disposition: Option (a) authorized by LA via Guide-#5

required_acknowledgments_for_ea_4_to_emi.ack: I will apply the 2-line _require_project_id strengthening in tools/_vikunja_client.py (extend rejection condition with `or project_id <= 0`; update error message to mention "positive int" / "not <= 0") and add the two new tests (test_require_project_id_rejects_zero, test_require_project_id_rejects_negative) to tools/tests/test_vikunja_client_scope.py. Re-run pytest tools/ and confirm 282 passed (was 280; +2 NET-ADD per V matrix). Refactor stays UNCOMMITTED on disk per the single-commit-at-2.9 protocol -- the f1 fix folds into the same commit at 2.9 alongside all other Stage 2 work. I will document f1 disposition + the LA authorization in the eventual 2.10 STATUS.md append-only entry.

I acknowledge the path-confusion correction: g5-4 disposition concerns `tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py` (the LEGACY pure-HTTP client) at Item 2.6; f1 disposition concerns `tools/_vikunja_client.py` (the NEW chokepoint, Stage 2.1 deliverable) at Item 2.4 closure. These are different files, different scope axes, and different dispositions. The 2.6 work plan does NOT inherently touch the chokepoint helper guard. f1 closure happens at 2.4; g5-4 closure happens at 2.6.

guide_review_response.continue_recommendation_unchanged: After the f1 fix lands and pytest 282 passed is confirmed, CONTINUE per my prior recommendation: Item 2.5 (canonical 7 PS1 with -BlarAIRoot per g4-2 + critical_notify.ps1 inspection per g4-1) and Item 2.5.v2 (per-PS1 verification). RE-EVALUATE budget honestly at the 2.5.v2 → 2.6 boundary. 2.6 is structurally comparable to file 13; if budget is healthy, push into 2.6; if even slightly tight, emit v4 mid-stage handoff per i1-4 / dn15 (docs-only commit, no partial-commit refactor).
guide_review_response.proceed_authorization: APPROVED with disposition Option (a). EA-4 may apply the f1 fix immediately upon receiving the LA's relay of this disposition (which IS the LA's explicit authorization for the deliberate scope expansion of fixing a Stage-2.1 file during 2.4 closure). After the fix + new tests + pytest 282 passed, EA-4 emits the two acknowledgments above (g5-5 + g5-6) and proceeds to Item 2.5 work without separate Guide re-review. Standing reminders unchanged: single Stage-2 commit at 2.9 (which now includes the f1 fix); fleet stays paused; no merge; stash@{0} untouched; append-only STATUS at 2.10 (which now includes f1 disposition log); g5-4 legacy file disposition still pending at 2.6 (unchanged); D3 dashboard_maintainer.py closure at 2.6 (unchanged); observation o1 handler-level-guard-vs-VikunjaClient-routing choice at 2.6 (unchanged). If the f1 fix surfaces unexpected test breakage (it should not per my disk verification), STOP and report. If pytest after fix shows fewer than 282 passed, investigate before proceeding.
